text.title: Productized Service Launch Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every Productized Service Launch artefact carries a single named owner (person, not 'team' / 'we'). Anonymous artefacts rejected.
rule.rationale: RACI: anonymous artefacts accumulate edits-by-anyone; rollback authority becomes unclear.

rule.statement: Every non-trivial field in the Productized Service Launch output is anchored to a verbatim citation (URL / ticket id / transcript id / metric snapshot). No anchor → field rejected as fabrication.
rule.rationale: LLMs cheerfully invent plausible values without inputs; verbatim anchoring makes the artefact falsifiable.

rule.statement: The Productized Service Launch template is versioned (semver). Each filled instance records the template_version it used so downstream consumers can re-parse legacy artefacts.
rule.rationale: Templates without version pinning become a one-way ratchet; old instances become unreadable when the template drifts.

rule.statement: No binding action (commit / send / charge / ramp / freeze) is taken on a Productized Service Launch output until a named human reviewer (not the producing agent) signs off.
rule.rationale: Self-approval defeats the audit trail; agent + reviewer is a two-key system on irreversible actions.

rule.statement: Every Productized Service Launch instance carries a last_touched ISO timestamp; instances with no touch in N cycles auto-archive instead of silently rotting.
rule.rationale: Un-owned, un-touched artefacts metastasize and the operator stops trusting the whole library.

rule.statement: Skip when: no approved design spec OR outbound list < 30 prospects OR beta cohort < 3. Methodology overhead does not pay back outside its preconditions.
rule.rationale: Scope guard: applying a methodology outside its preconditions produces noise without signal.
